content.problemStatement:
To curb the declining literacy in Chefland, the government has come up with a new plan.

You are given an array AAA of size NNN denoting the initial count of literate people in each of the NNN states of Chefland.

The government wants the count of literate people in each state to be **at least** MMM.

To do this, the government can combine two or more **consecutive** states to represent a new state. The number of literate people in the new state will be equal to the sum of literate people in the individual consecutive states that were combined to create this new state.

Find the **maximum** number of states the government can have while maintaining the condition that **at least** MMM literate people are present in each state.

Note that it is guaranteed that MMM is not more than the sum of AiA_iAi​.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM — the number of states initially and and the minimum number of literate people required in each state.
The next line consists of NNN space-separated integers denoting the initial count of literate people in each state.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the **maximum** number of states the government can have while maintaining the condition of **at least** MMM literate people in each state.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases ≤ 2·10^5
It is guaranteed that M is not larger than the total sum of Ai in each test case.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case starts with a line containing two integers N and M – the number of states and the required minimum literate people per state. The next line contains N space‑separated integers A1 … AN, the initial literate counts of the states.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with the maximum possible number of states after merging consecutive states so that every resulting state has at least M literate people.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: Every individual state already has at least 2 literate people, so we keep all three states.\nTest case 2: The total sum is 9 which is ≥5, but no prefix reaches 5, therefore we must merge all three states into one.\nTest case 3: Greedy merging gives [3+7]=10 and [8+2]=10, two states.

solution.approach:
All Ai are positive. To obtain the maximum number of groups we should close a group as soon as its sum reaches at least M – making each group as small as possible. Scan the array, keep a running sum cur. Whenever cur ≥ M we have formed one valid state, increment the answer and reset cur to 0. After processing all elements, if no group was formed (answer == 0) the whole array itself forms one valid state because the total sum ≥ M. Any leftover sum < M at the end is simply merged with the previous group, which does not change the count. This greedy procedure yields the optimal answer.
Time Complexity: O(N) per test case.
Space Complexity: O(1) besides input storage.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the length of the array in that test case.
